metadata.title:
WINS POINT FOR
:: DAY LABOR

NEW WATER MAIN DEAL PUT
ON BASIS THAT WILL PAY
$2.75 AFTER COMPANY HAD
FIGURED $2.25.

pages.raw_text:
Citizen labor won a hot fight in
the council last night against the
Homesite Land Co., represented by
Attorney Alex Winston. This COM
pany has been figuring on putting
in water mains at a cost of about
$4,000, and last night on the
recommendation of the finance
committee, the council entered into
"a new deal and agreed to put in
the mains by day labor at $2.75 a
day, the city to be reimbursed by
the company. One year after the
revenues from the system reach IL
per cent. the city will purchase the
mains.


The day albor deal was what the
company did not desire. Thomas
Maloney of Federal union No. H.
624 for several weeks was decker
ing with the company for the job
of laying the mains. He offered to
save the contractors profit. The
company was willing to accept pro-
vided Maloney did not pay his men
more than 1925 a day, claiming
that men could be got for that, and
even less.


When the proposition reached
the council last night, Councilmen
Baldwin, Dalke and Funk made
hard fight against establishing
precedent which would bring other
companies in with like schemes,
when the council would be forced
to treat all alike.


For many years the custom has
been for the city to purchase mains
out Of the revenues. laying aside
the receipts until they reached
